fact: October 30 is exactly 61 days away from the end of the year - that's like counting down from your birthday to Christmas!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now, hiding up to 10,000 acorns each before winter hits!
The sun sets before 6 PM in most of North America on this date, making it feel like bedtime when you're still doing homework.
October 30 falls smack in the middle of peak leaf-peeping season, when trees show off their brightest reds, oranges, and yellows before going bald for winter.
Bats are having their last big feeding frenzy before hibernation, gobbling up thousands of mosquitoes and moths in a single night.
The morning frost starts appearing on car windshields around this time in colder regions, creating sparkly ice crystals that look like nature's glitter.
October 30 is a Scorpio date, which means people born today are supposedly mysterious and intense - perfect timing for Halloween vibes!
Spiders are at their absolute biggest and fattest right now because they've been eating bugs all summer long, preparing for their winter slowdown.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, October 30 is prime springtime - flowers are blooming while the rest of us are raking dead leaves!
Deer are in the middle of their mating season, making them extra jumpy and likely to bound across roads at weird times.
The moon looks extra spooky and orange when it rises around this date because of all the dust and particles floating in the crisp autumn air.
